# Mission: Dogfood: canonical implement-uh-slice mission template

Create the canonical mission template that UH itself will use to implement any future UH slice (one Linear UH-NNN ticket = one mission). This is the first dogfood-mode mission: UH-on-UH. Success here proves the team mission shape (UH-72) + staged workflow profile (UH-73) can produce a usable artifact end-to-end without a human in the implementation loop. Failure here is itself the most diagnostic information available — it tells us which of the six gaps documented in the v0.8.0 post-mortem actually bite.
Deliverables:
  1. examples/missions/templates/implement-uh-slice.yaml — a parameterized
     canonical mission packet that another mission author can copy and
     fill in for any UH-NNN slice. MUST use `shape: team`,
     `workflow_profile: staged`, complete `acceptance_criteria[]` block,
     and `verification.required_checks[]` that enforce typecheck + tests
     at minimum.
  2. examples/missions/templates/README.md — one-page operator guide:
     "Copy this when implementing a UH-NNN Linear slice. Substitute the
     <ALL_CAPS> placeholders. Then `uh mission run-team <id>`."

Non-goals:
  - Implementing any real UH-NNN slice via the template (that's the next
    mission, not this one).
  - Adding `uh mission promote --pr` or Linear-state-flip (those are
    separate slices flagged in the post-mortem).
  - Changing the mission schema, staged workflow profile, or team-run.ts
    runtime. The template MUST be expressible under today's schema.

## Workflow: Staged (Plan → PRD → Execute → Verify → Fix)

### plan (architect)
Produce a plan.md outlining the approach before any code lands.

### prd (product-manager)
Capture the contract / scope / acceptance in prd.md.

### execute (developer)
Implement the change. For team missions, workers fan out in parallel.

### verify (reviewer)
Run mission verification against the executed result.

### fix (developer)
Address verification findings. Loops back into verify up to mission.verification.max_iterations times.

## Related Issues
- [linear] UH-126 (https://linear.app/agenticengineering-agency/team/UH/active)

## Read First
- src/schema/mission.ts
- src/harness/team-run.ts
- .harness/workflows/staged.yaml
- examples/missions/documentation-spine.yaml
- examples/missions/codex-e2e-smoke.yaml
- tests/team-schema.test.ts
- docs/ROADMAP.md
- docs/prds/uh-roadmap-to-1.0-v1.0-prd.md

## Expected Artifacts
- examples/missions/templates/implement-uh-slice.yaml
- examples/missions/templates/README.md

## Verification Checks
- bun run typecheck
- bun -e 'import("./src/schema/mission.ts").then(async m => { const yaml = await import("yaml"); const fs = await import("node:fs/promises"); const doc = yaml.parse(await fs.readFile("examples/missions/templates/implement-uh-slice.yaml", "utf8")); m.validateMission(doc); console.log("OK"); })'
- test -f examples/missions/templates/README.md

Execute this mission and produce the expected artifacts.

## Runtime final message

At the very end of your response, emit your one-paragraph summary inside a fenced code block tagged `uh-runtime-final-message`:

```uh-runtime-final-message
<one-paragraph summary of what you did, what changed, and any caveats>
```

This fenced block MUST be the last block in your output. The harness extracts its content verbatim into `runtime-final.txt`.

schema_version: uh.workflow.v0
id: staged
name: Staged (Plan → PRD → Execute → Verify → Fix)
description: >-
  Five-phase staged workflow used by the runStagedWorkflow runtime. Plan
  and PRD establish the contract before any code lands; Execute dispatches
  to the mission's adapter(s); Verify runs the structured verification
  pipeline; Fix is bounded by mission.verification.max_iterations and
  loops back into Verify on a failed result. For shape=team missions the
  leader runs Plan/PRD/Verify/Fix and workers fan out for Execute via
  UH-72.
bmad:
  inspiration: BMAD-METHOD
  dependency: false
  roles:
    - Analyst
    - Product Manager
    - Architect
    - Scrum Master / Workflow Designer
    - Developer
    - QA / Test Architect
    - Technical Writer
  guardrails:
    - BMAD is inspiration only, not a runtime dependency.
    - Roles are hats; a single agent may play multiple roles.
    - Outputs should be traceable and reviewable.
    - Do not import BMAD-METHOD as a dependency.
phases:
  - name: plan
    agent_role: architect
    bmad_role: Architect
    description: Produce a plan.md outlining the approach before any code lands.
    outputs:
      - plan.md
  - name: prd
    agent_role: product-manager
    bmad_role: Product Manager
    description: Capture the contract / scope / acceptance in prd.md.
    outputs:
      - prd.md
  - name: execute
    agent_role: developer
    bmad_role: Developer
    description: Implement the change. For team missions, workers fan out in parallel.
    outputs:
      - runtime-final.txt
      - integration-report.md
  - name: verify
    agent_role: reviewer
    bmad_role: QA / Test Architect
    description: Run mission verification against the executed result.
    outputs:
      - verify-report.md
  - name: fix
    agent_role: developer
    bmad_role: Developer
    description: Address verification findings. Loops back into verify up to mission.verification.max_iterations times.
    outputs:
      - fix-report.md

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
A reusable template has two audiences: the YAML parser and the future operator. Fields constrained by enums, like adapter ids, need concrete valid defaults; placeholders are safer in free-text fields, artifact paths, commands, and Linear references.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

I’m adding the artifacts now. The template will default to `codex` leader/workers so it validates immediately, while the README explains which placeholders to replace before running a real UH-NNN mission.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The template keeps `tdd.enforce_tests_first: true` because this mission class is meant for implementation slices, not ad hoc documentation. I also duplicated artifact existence as both acceptance criteria and required checks so operators get human-readable contract language plus executable gates.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

The files are in place. I’m running the requested validation now, plus the project test command because the template itself makes tests a required check for future slice missions.

# Implement UH Slice Mission Template

Copy `implement-uh-slice.yaml` when implementing one Linear UH-NNN slice as a team mission.

## How To Use

1. Copy `examples/missions/templates/implement-uh-slice.yaml` to a mission-specific file or mission packet location.
2. Substitute every `<ALL_CAPS>` placeholder:
   - `<UH-NNN>`: the Linear issue id, for example `UH-126`.
   - `<SLICE_NAME>`: a short human-readable slice name.
   - `<PRIMARY_CODE_OR_DOC_PATH>` and `<SECONDARY_CODE_OR_DOC_PATH>`: the most important files the mission agents should read first.
   - `<EXPECTED_ARTIFACT_PATH>`: the primary file the slice must create or modify.
   - `<EXPECTED_TEST_PATH>`: the targeted regression test file for the slice.
3. Adjust `team.workers` only if the slice needs different valid adapter ids or more parallel lanes.
4. Keep `shape: team`, `workflow_profile: staged`, `acceptance_criteria[]`, and the typecheck/test entries in `verification.required_checks[]`.
5. Run the mission:

```sh
uh mission run-team <id>
```

## Operator Notes

The template is intentionally valid under today's schema before placeholder substitution. Adapter ids use concrete defaults because the schema validates them against the registered team adapters. Placeholder values are reserved for mission-specific text, paths, commands, and Linear references.

Use this for implementation missions only. Do not use it to promote PRs, flip Linear state, or implement multiple UH slices in one packet.

schema_version: uh.mission.v0
id: <UH-NNN>-implement-slice
title: <UH-NNN> Implement <SLICE_NAME>
workflow_profile: staged
priority: high
objective: >-
  Implement the <UH-NNN> Linear slice end-to-end under the staged workflow.
  Produce a plan and PRD first, execute the scoped change in a team mission,
  verify with required checks, and address verification findings within the
  bounded fix loop.
issue_refs:
  - provider: linear
    id: <UH-NNN>
    url: https://linear.app/agenticengineering-agency/issue/<UH-NNN>
context:
  repo_root: .
  read_first:
    - src/schema/mission.ts
    - src/harness/team-run.ts
    - .harness/workflows/staged.yaml
    - <PRIMARY_CODE_OR_DOC_PATH>
    - <SECONDARY_CODE_OR_DOC_PATH>
  source_links:
    - https://linear.app/agenticengineering-agency/issue/<UH-NNN>
constraints:
  - Keep the change expressible under today's mission schema and runtime.
  - Keep diffs small, reviewable, and scoped to <UH-NNN>.
  - Do not implement unrelated slices or change Linear promotion behavior.
  - Preserve existing public contracts unless the PRD explicitly narrows the change.
skills:
  suggested:
    - debug-like-expert
    - lint
shape: team
team:
  leader:
    adapter: codex
    role: integrator
  workers:
    - adapter: codex
      role: implementation
      count: 1
    - adapter: codex
      role: verification
      count: 1
integration_report_path: .harness/missions/<UH-NNN>-implement-slice/team/integration-report.md
expected_outputs:
  files:
    - <EXPECTED_ARTIFACT_PATH>
    - <EXPECTED_TEST_PATH>
completion_criteria:
  - "plan.md exists and explains the implementation approach before code changes."
  - "prd.md captures scope, non-goals, acceptance criteria, and verification contract."
  - "<EXPECTED_ARTIFACT_PATH> implements the <UH-NNN> slice."
  - "<EXPECTED_TEST_PATH> covers the changed behavior."
  - "All required verification checks pass."
acceptance_criteria:
  - id: plan-artifact
    description: "The staged plan phase produced plan.md before implementation work."
    check_command: "test -f .harness/missions/<UH-NNN>-implement-slice/plan.md"
    severity: block
  - id: prd-artifact
    description: "The staged PRD phase produced prd.md with scope, non-goals, and acceptance criteria."
    check_command: "test -f .harness/missions/<UH-NNN>-implement-slice/prd.md"
    severity: block
  - id: expected-artifact
    description: "The slice's primary expected artifact exists and is ready for review."
    check_command: "test -f <EXPECTED_ARTIFACT_PATH>"
    severity: block
  - id: regression-coverage
    description: "The slice includes targeted regression coverage for the changed behavior."
    check_command: "test -f <EXPECTED_TEST_PATH>"
    severity: block
  - id: required-checks-pass
    description: "Typecheck and test commands pass before the mission is complete."
    check_command: "bun run typecheck && bun run test"
    severity: block
tdd:
  enforce_tests_first: true
  test_paths:
    - tests/**
    - "**/*.test.ts"
    - "**/*.spec.ts"
  source_paths:
    - src/**
    - examples/**
runtime_requirements:
  needs_network: false
  needs_shell: true
  needs_fs_write: true
  max_cost_class: premium
verification:
  max_iterations: 2
  required_checks:
    - name: typecheck
      command: bun run typecheck
    - name: tests
      command: bun run test
    - name: expected artifact exists
      command: test -f <EXPECTED_ARTIFACT_PATH>
    - name: expected test exists
      command: test -f <EXPECTED_TEST_PATH>
  review_gates:
    - "Reviewer confirms the PRD contract and implementation diff match <UH-NNN>."
    - "Reviewer confirms no non-goal slice or promotion behavior was implemented."

The non-bundled loader gets the suite past Vite startup, but many existing CLI/worktree tests fail in this restricted worker layout. I’ll let it finish so the final report has the real failure class instead of a partial claim.
